rationale: The page contains specific information regarding the 'e-Zero FIR' initiative by the Ministry of Home Affairs and the legal definition/provisions of a 'Zero FIR'. The bottom half of the page is an advertisement which has been excluded from the cleaned text.
cleanedText:
4.5.2. E-ZERO FIR
MHA's Indian Cybercrime Coordination Centre (I4C) has introduced the new e-Zero FIR initiative.
About e-Zero FIR
- Launched as a pilot project for Delhi.
- Aim: ease reporting and prompt action on complaints related to cyber financial crimes.
- Threshold: Financial losses above threshold limit of Rs. 10 lakh made to National Cybercrime Reporting Portal (NCRP) or 1930 helpline will automatically lead to registration of a Zero FIR with e-Crime Police Station of Delhi.
- This will be immediately routed to the territorial Cyber Crime Police Stations.
- Complainants can visit cybercrime Police Station within 3 days and get the Zero FIR converted into a regular FIR.
About Zero FIR
- Zero FIR is the mode of logging FIR in any Police Station irrespective of the offence committed in that area or any other area of jurisdiction.
- The provision for Zero FIR is under Section 173 (1) of Bharatiya Nagrik Suraksha Sanhita, 2023, based on Justice Verma Committee's recommendation established after 2012 Nirbhaya case.
